ctxt:
Welcome to sealifeandmore.com!

Inspired by my desire to share great video and image links (and a few of my own images and videos mostly from the fabulous Monterey Bay Aquarium), as well as my desire to put images in a beautiful and conceivably useful form

Learning how to navigate.

To start:

Swipe right on desktop means:

--> Hold down left mouse button.

--> While holding down button, move mouse briskly to the right a short distance.

--> Then release left mouse button.

"Swipe right" on cells in grid will generally bring up larger text or images.

The upper right cell often helps instruct, initially in how to use options of the site,

later in helping learn content. Swipe right activates it!

Some navigation instructions are shown below, but better to learn by doing.

Help!

In Gridveiw, swipe right on cell for full screen.

Once full screen:

Sizing on computer:

- mouse hold zooms and held mouse pans.  Control-hold-mouse reduces zoom.  Double-click goes to 1:1

Sizing on phone/tablet:

- two fingers separate to zoom, two fingers pan, pinch to reduce zoom.  Tap to go to 1:1

To get back to gridview:

- Swipe left on desktop or phone.


Slideshows!

in Gridview, on desktop type s, on phone use menu.

Shows images from sealifeandmore.com as an adjustable slide show.

Local disk shows your option for playing from your own image files (probably available on Chrome and Edge browsers on Windows computers, possibly available on other browsers.)

My other websites: 

https://www.sealifeandmore.org
A Wordpress site, still the best place to see Instagram videos

https://pwassm.github.io/braintrain
My first site, exploring double decision paradigm..

Sheephead eats sea urchin

Last frame: Garabaldi takes a bite, too.

Purple sea urchins can live up to 40 years, this one was pretty big,
big enough for a California sheephead's lunch!

Table tennis fun

Table tennis fun!

amazing rallies

pres:
Welcome! The Radula collection

The radula is the ribbon of tiny teeth that snails, chitons and their kin eat with. This grid mixes films and photographs of it.

One key does most of the work: → (right arrow) turns to the next page, ← goes back.


Now press the → key in order to turn the page.

Step 1 — open the reader

These lessons are easier to read full screen.

Now press the ↑ key (up arrow) in order to open the full-screen reader. Then press → again.

A mouse works too: hold the left button anywhere in this box, drag to the RIGHT, and let go — a "swipe right" opens the same reader. The arrow keys work in the small box too, so you can always keep reading.

Step 2 — the reader

You made it. Inside the reader you can turn pages three ways: the ‹ and › arrows at the edges, the → / ← keys, or a swipe left / right (drag left = next page, drag right = previous). The ↓ key (down arrow) — or the red ✕, or Esc — closes the reader and puts the little square back, still on this very page; ↑ from the grid opens it again.

Now try each one: press → and ← a few times, then swipe left and right inside this reader, in order to feel how pages turn. Then press → to go on.

Step 3 — hidden notes & the big blue triangle

Some pages tuck extra detail behind a big blue triangle ▶. Click the triangle to open the note; click it again to close (while open it tips over to point down ▼).

Click this blue triangle now

Perfect — that is all there is to it. Look for the ▶ triangle anywhere on this site; it always means "there is more here."


Now click the triangle above in order to practice, and then press → for a surprise: the next page is a LIVE video.

(If the next page shows only the letters "1b", you are reading in the small square — press ↑ first to open the reader.)

1b

Step 4 — that was a real video!

That page was square 1b — a gumboot chiton grinding away with its radula — playing right inside the lesson. On any video page:

  • Space = pause / play · M = sound off / on

The → and ← keys keep turning pages here too — a video page is no different. One extra trick, just for video pages: hold Shift while pressing them — Shift+→ / Shift+← — to STEP the film one frame at a time (wonderful for a fast-moving creature).

Now press ← to visit the video again, press Space to pause it, press Shift+→ a few times to step through frames, press M for sound — then press → in order to come back here.

Step 5 — pausing videos on the grid

Back on the grid, every square plays on its own. Two controls:

  • Swipe LEFT on a video square = pause that film (swipe left again = play).
  • Space = pause or play ALL the squares at once.

(Swipe left on THIS text square instead turns to the next lesson page — there is no film here to pause.)

Now press Esc in order to reach the grid, swipe left on any moving square to pause it, and press Space a couple of times. Return by pressing ↑ — the reader reopens right here.

Step 6 — any square, full screen

Swipe RIGHT on any square to watch it full screen. For a film: Space = pause / play · M = sound off / on · ← / → = one frame at a time · hold the LEFT mouse button = zoom in · drag = look around · double-click = zoom out · Esc = back.

Now press Esc, swipe right on any film square, pause it with Space, step with →, and hold the left button to zoom. Return with Esc, then press ↑ to come back here. Press → for a full-screen PHOTO next.

3e

Step 7 — looking at the photographs

This collection mixes films (the top rows) with photographs (the lower rows) — that page was square 3e, the surprise hidden in a limpet’s teeth. Pictures zoom just like films: hold the LEFT mouse button to zoom in (slowly, then faster), drag to move around, double-click to reset, Esc to go back.

Now press ← to the photo, hold the left button to zoom deep into the teeth, drag around, double-click to reset — then press → to return here.

Step 8 — getting around the site

  • Esc from the grid — or a swipe LEFT carried ACROSS the edge of a square — = the Main Page: greeting, Search, and every collection. That is also where you choose a different collection.
  • G with the mouse over a film = that film’s own YouTube or Vimeo page, in a new tab, with the author’s notes.
  • H = Help, any time · 0 (zero) = fill your whole monitor (press 0 again to shrink) · Shift+C = captions on/off for every film.

Now press → in order to peek at the grid THROUGH the lesson.

G

Step 9 — a window onto the grid

That see-through page shows the grid still playing underneath. Look, don’t touch — it wakes up again when you press Esc.

Now press → in order to learn the slideshow — the nicest way to lean back and enjoy the whole collection.

Step 10 — the slideshow

The slideshow turns the whole grid into a full-screen show that advances on its own.

  1. Press Esc to the grid, then press S.
  2. A settings panel opens. Set Show to Both so the films play in among the photographs.
  3. Click Start ▶▶ and enjoy.

During the show: → or Space = next now · ← = previous · Esc = end · the small + at the right edge brings the settings back.

Now press Esc, press S, set Show to Both, and click Start ▶▶ in order to watch. End with Esc and return here.

Step 11 — slideshow options

Open the settings (press S, or the + during a show) and explore:

  • Each slide — seconds each slide stays · Show — Image / Video / Both / Both-ShortVid.
  • Order — IN ORDER or RANDOM · Loop — start over at the end.
  • Zoom & Pan — a slow, gentle drift into each picture · Transition — the cross-fade · Delay — a pause before the drift.
  • Title / Comment — each slide’s name and notes, in the size you like · CanvasBlur — a soft backdrop behind small pictures.

Your settings are remembered on this computer.

Now open the settings, set Order to RANDOM, and Start ▶▶ in order to see the difference. Return with Esc.

Step 12 — playtime, and leaving

  • This is a big 25-square grid, so two toys work here: R = the conveyor — the outer ring of squares glides around while the films keep playing (press R again to stop) · F = falling cells (F again to stop).
  • ] / [ = zoom the whole grid in / out · Ctrl+] / Ctrl+[ = zoom one square · Z = back to normal.

Leaving a collection: swipe LEFT and carry the swipe ACROSS the edge of a square — from one square into the next — to return to the Grid Choice / Main Page, where you pick another collection. Pressing Esc does the same.

Congratulations — you can drive the whole site. Press Esc and enjoy, or press ← any time to revisit a lesson.

Cheat sheet — every control on one page

Grid: ↑ = open the lesson reader · swipe right on a square = full screen · swipe left on a film = pause · Space = pause all · S = slideshow · G over a film = its home page · Esc or swipe left across a square’s edge = Main Page & collections · H = help · 0 = fill monitor · Shift+C = captions · ] [ = zoom · Z = reset · R = conveyor · F = fall.

Full screen: Space = pause · M = sound · ← → = frame step (video) · hold left button = zoom · double-click = zoom out · Esc = back.

Reader (lesson pages): ‹ › / → ← / swipe = turn pages, video pages included (Shift+→ ← = frame step there) · ↓, ✕ or Esc = back to the grid.

Slideshow: S to open · Show: Both · Start ▶▶ · → / ← = next / previous · + = settings · Esc = end.

Leave: swipe left across a square’s edge, or Esc → Main Page.

Welcome! Learn to drive this site

One key does most of the work.

The → key (right arrow) turns to the next page. The ← key goes back.


Now press the → key in order to turn the page.

Step 1 — open the reader

These lessons are easier to read full screen.

Now press the ↑ key (up arrow) in order to open the full-screen reader. Then press → again.

A mouse works too: hold the left button anywhere in this box, drag to the RIGHT, and let go — a "swipe right" opens the same reader. The arrow keys work in the small box too, so you can always keep reading.

Step 2 — the reader

You made it. Inside the reader you can turn pages three ways: the ‹ and › arrows at the edges, the → / ← keys, or a swipe left / right (drag left = next page, drag right = previous). The ↓ key (down arrow) — or the red ✕, or Esc — closes the reader and puts the little square back, still on this very page; ↑ from the grid opens it again.

Now press → and ← a few times, then swipe left and right inside this reader, in order to feel how pages turn. Then press → to go on.

Step 3 — hidden notes & the big blue triangle

Some pages tuck extra detail behind a big blue triangle ▶. Click the triangle to open the note; click it again to close (while open it tips over to point down ▼).

Click this blue triangle now

Perfect — that is all there is to it. The ▶ triangle always means "there is more here."


Now click the triangle above in order to practice, and then press → for a surprise: the next page is a LIVE video.

(If the next page shows only "2b", you are in the small square — press ↑ first.)

2b

Step 4 — that was a real video!

That page was square 2b — the shag-rug nudibranch — playing inside the lesson. On any video page:

  • Space = pause / play · M = sound off / on

The → and ← keys keep turning pages here too — a video page is no different. One extra trick, just for video pages: hold Shift while pressing them — Shift+→ / Shift+← — to STEP the video one frame at a time (wonderful for a fast-moving creature).

Now press ← to the video, press Space to pause, press Shift+→ to step frames, press M for sound — then press → to return here.

Step 5 — pausing videos on the grid

Back on the grid, every square plays on its own. Two controls:

  • Swipe LEFT on a video square = pause that video (swipe left again = play).
  • Space = pause or play ALL the squares at once.

(Swipe left on THIS text square turns to the next lesson page instead.)

Now press Esc in order to reach the grid, swipe left on any video to pause it, and press Space a couple of times. Return by pressing ↑ — the reader reopens right here.

Step 6 — any square, full screen

The same swipe right works on every square.

Full screen: Space = pause / play · M = sound off / on · ← / → = one frame at a time · hold the LEFT mouse button = zoom in · drag = look around · double-click = zoom out · Esc = back to the grid.

Now press Esc, swipe right on the seahorse video (bottom-right square), pause it with Space, step frames with →, and hold the left button to zoom. Return with Esc, then press ↑ to come back here.

Step 7 — getting around the site

  • Esc from the grid — or a swipe LEFT carried ACROSS the edge of a square — = the Main Page: greeting, Search, and every collection. That is also where you choose a different collection.
  • G with the mouse over a video = that video’s own YouTube / Vimeo page, in a new tab.
  • H = Help · 0 = fill your whole monitor · Shift+C = captions on/off.

Now press → in order to peek at the grid THROUGH the lesson.

G

Step 8 — a window onto the grid

That see-through page shows the grid still playing underneath. Look, don’t touch — it wakes up again when you press Esc.

Now press → in order to learn the slideshow.

Step 9 — the slideshow

The slideshow turns the whole grid into a full-screen show that advances on its own.

  1. Press Esc to the grid, then press S.
  2. Set Show to Both (this collection is all videos — include them!).
  3. Click Start ▶▶ and enjoy.

During the show: → or Space = next now · ← = previous · Esc = end · the small + at the right edge brings the settings back.

Now press Esc, press S, set Show to Both, and click Start ▶▶ in order to watch. End with Esc and return here.

Step 10 — slideshow options

Open the settings (press S, or the + during a show) and explore:

  • Each slide — seconds each slide stays · Show — Image / Video / Both / Both-ShortVid.
  • Order — IN ORDER or RANDOM · Loop — start over at the end.
  • Zoom & Pan — a slow drift into each picture · Transition — the cross-fade · Delay — a pause first.
  • Title / Comment captions · CanvasBlur — a soft backdrop.

Your settings are remembered on this computer.

Now set Order to RANDOM and Start ▶▶ in order to see the difference. Return with Esc.

Step 11 — playtime, and leaving

  • On the BIG collections (25 or 17 squares — pick one from the Main Page): R = the conveyor (the ring of squares glides around, videos still playing) · F = falling cells. Same key again stops it.
  • ] / [ = zoom the whole grid · Ctrl+] / Ctrl+[ = zoom one square · Z = reset.

Leaving a collection: swipe LEFT and carry the swipe ACROSS the edge of a square — from one square into the next — to return to the Grid Choice / Main Page, where you pick another collection. Pressing Esc does the same.

Congratulations — you can drive the whole site. Press Esc and enjoy, or press ← to revisit a lesson.

Cheat sheet — every control on one page

Grid: ↑ = open the lesson reader · swipe right on a square = full screen · swipe left = pause · Space = pause all · S = slideshow · G over a video = its home page · Esc or swipe left across a square’s edge = Main Page & collections · H = help · 0 = fill monitor · Shift+C = captions.

Full screen: Space = pause · M = sound · ← → = frame step · hold left button = zoom · double-click = zoom out · Esc = back.

Reader: ‹ › / → ← / swipe = turn pages, video pages included (Shift+→ ← = frame step there) · ↓, ✕ or Esc = back to the grid.

Slideshow: S · Show: Both · Start ▶▶ · → / ← = next / previous · + = settings · Esc = end.

Leave: swipe left across a square’s edge, or Esc → Main Page.

Welcome!

These little pages teach you everything, one tiny step at a time.


Now press the → key (right arrow) on your keyboard.

It worked!

The → key turns the page. The ← key goes back.

Now press ← once, and then → once, in order to practice. Then press → again to go on.

Make it big

Reading is nicer full screen.

Now press the ↑ key (up arrow) in order to open the big reader. Then press → again.

(A mouse works too: hold the left button inside this box, drag to the RIGHT, and let go.) Inside the reader you can turn pages with the ‹ › arrows, the → ← keys, or a swipe. To leave the reader: press the ↓ key, or Esc, or click the red ✕ — the little square keeps your page.

A hidden note

Some pages hide extra words behind a big blue triangle ▶.

Click this blue triangle

Well done! The ▶ triangle always means "there is more here." It tips down ▼ while it is open.


Now click the triangle in order to practice, and then press →.

About this collection

Seventeen short films of reef life by Coral Morphologic. The big wide square is the porcelain crab.

Now press → in order to see the porcelain crab film RIGHT HERE inside the lesson.

(If the next page shows only "1L", press ↑ first to open the reader.)

1L

You just watched square 1L

On a film page: Space = pause and play · M = sound on and off.

The → and ← keys keep turning pages here too — a film page is no different. One extra trick, just for film pages: hold Shift while pressing them — Shift+→ / Shift+← — to STEP the film one frame at a time.

Now press ← to the film, press Space, press Space again — then press → to come back.

Try it on the grid

Two mouse moves work on every square of the grid:

  • Drag LEFT on a film square = pause it (drag left again = play).
  • Drag RIGHT on a square = watch it full screen. Esc = back.

Now press Esc to reach the grid and try both moves on any square. Return by pressing ↑ — the reader reopens right here.

The slideshow

The site can show you the whole collection by itself, full screen.

Now press Esc, then press the S key. In the panel, set Show to Both, and click Start ▶▶ in order to begin. When you have watched enough, press Esc.

During the show: → = next film now · ← = previous · the little + at the right edge opens the settings again.

Playtime

This is a 17-square grid, so two toys work here:

  • R = the conveyor: the squares ride around the edge while they play. R again stops it.
  • F = falling cells. F again stops it.

Now press Esc and then R in order to watch the squares go for a ride.

That’s everything!

Press H any time for the full Help screen. Esc shows the Main Page with every collection.

Leaving a collection: swipe LEFT and carry the swipe ACROSS the edge of a square — from one square into the next — to return to the Grid Choice / Main Page, where you pick another collection. Pressing Esc does the same.

Little cheat sheet

↑ = open the reader · ↓ = leave the reader · drag right on a square = full screen · drag left = pause · Space = pause all · S = slideshow · Esc = back / Main Page · H = help · 0 = fill the whole monitor.
